title: poa helenae
intro: Poa helenae, also known as Helen's meadow grass or Helen's blue grass, is a species of grass native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Poa helenae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
